title: GTM Engineer
description: Glean is seeking a GTM Engineer to be the technical backbone of its go-to-market engine. The role involves systematizing and automating GTM workflows by designing scalable processes for enrichment, scoring, routing and outreach across the customer journey, accelerating pipeline generation through innovative scaled outreach, and running data-driven experiments. Requires a proven track record designing and shipping GTM workflows that move the needle on pipeline, conversion or retention, experience with AI-native automation tools (particularly Clay), and deep understanding of B2B SaaS funnels and GTM motions. Base salary $170,000–$200,000.
score_reason: Exceptional match—Omri's 15+ years in B2B SaaS RevOps/GTM aligns perfectly with Glean's need for a technical GTM architect. His expertise in pipeline forecasting, workflow automation, and data-driven GTM motions directly addresses the role's core requirements around scoring, routing, and scaled outreach.
ai_opener: Omri Gonen here—I've spent 15+ years building the RevOps and GTM engines at B2B SaaS companies, and I'm genuinely impressed by Glean's approach to systematizing pipeline generation at scale. Your GTM Engineer role caught my attention because I've designed and shipped exactly these kinds of workflows (enrichment, scoring, routing, outreach automation) that move needle metrics—and I'm currently exploring roles where I can architect AI-native GTM systems like the ones you're building.

title: GTM Engineer
description: As a GTM Engineer at MaintainX, you'll scale pipeline generation by leveraging automated GTM systems and AI to drive pipeline via automated outbounding, researching new outbound strategies and tools. The role reports to the VP of Demand Generation and collaborates closely with Revenue Operations, Sales and Marketing. Requires 2–5+ years in GTM, Marketing Ops, Sales Ops, or Revenue Operations with a technical focus, hands-on experience with tools like Clay, Salesforce, Zapier, and Outreach, email marketing knowledge, and a strong data-driven mindset.
score_reason: Strong alignment with GTM Engineer focus, AI-native systems, and pipeline automation expertise. However, the 2-5 year requirement and reporting to VP Demand Gen (vs. direct RevOps leadership) is slightly junior relative to Omri's 15+ year senior executive background and typical Head of RevOps target roles.
ai_opener: I've been scaling GTM infrastructure and AI-driven pipeline systems for B2B SaaS companies for 15+ years, with deep expertise in Salesforce, automation stacks (Zapier, Clay), and data-driven outbound strategies. MaintainX's focus on automated GTM systems and AI-powered pipeline generation aligns perfectly with how I've architected revenue infrastructure at scale—and I'm energized by hands-on technical GTM engineering work alongside your Revenue Operations team.

title: GTM Engineer
description: Boulevard is hiring a GTM Engineer to architect and scale the systems that power outbound, routing, enrichment, and seller workflows as it scales from $50M toward $250M in revenue. Reporting to the Director of Revenue Operations, you'll design and maintain integrations across Salesforce, Clay, LeanData, Gong, and Outreach. Requires 3–5 years in GTM Operations, Revenue Operations, Sales Operations, or a technical automation role within B2B SaaS, deep Salesforce experience, and hands-on workflow building in Clay and LeanData. Base salary $99,000–$149,000.
score_reason: Exceptional match for senior RevOps executive—GTM Engineer role at $50M→$250M scale aligns perfectly with Omri's 15+ years B2B SaaS expertise, Salesforce mastery, and pipeline/workflow architecture background. Remote US location meets his global remote flexibility, and the technical depth (Clay, LeanData, Outreach stack) matches his hands-on systems design skillset.
ai_opener: Boulevard's GTM Engine is solving a critical scaling challenge—moving from $50M to $250M requires bulletproof routing, enrichment, and seller workflows across your entire stack. With 15+ years architecting these systems at scale across Salesforce, Clay, LeanData, and similar platforms, I've seen exactly where most teams stumble (usually in data flow and automation governance). I'd love to explore how we can avoid those patterns and build something that actually scales with you.

title: RevOps Expert
description: Remote is looking for a RevOps Expert to join its Revenue Operations team and lead optimization of sales processes and system architecture. You'll drive strategic initiatives aligned with the go-to-market strategy, champion scalable process design, and implement solutions using Six Sigma and Agile. Requires extensive operations management experience (revenue, CS or sales domains), experience with tools like LeanData, Zapier or Camunda, deep Salesforce proficiency, proven SaaS and B2B experience, and experience working in a remote environment. All positions are fully remote.

Your focus on strategic RevOps initiatives and process standardization aligns perfectly with how I approach GTM enablement—I'd be interested in exploring how my experience driving pipeline forecasting and system optimization could support Remote's revenue growth.
